paper_title: Analysis of angles measured on the surface of Hibiscus rosa-sinensis pollen grain

methods.description: This method involves measuring the angles between spines on the surface of Hibiscus rosa-sinensis pollen grains to compare experimental results with theoretical predictions and to understand the symmetry and structure of the pollen grain.

steps.description: Consider only experimental measurements from the length of the spines in the periphery of the grain.
steps.searchable_summary: filter measurements to include only spine lengths from the periphery of the pollen grain

steps.description: Use computational calculations to obtain the lengths of the angles between spines.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate angles between spines using computational methods

steps.description: Measure 100 angles, with two-thirds subtended by hexagonal triangles and the rest by pentagonal ones.
steps.searchable_summary: measure and categorize 100 angles into hexagonal and pentagonal groups

steps.description: Calculate the mean value and standard deviation for hexagons and pentagons.
steps.searchable_summary: compute mean and standard deviation for hexagonal and pentagonal angles

steps.description: Compare experimental results with theoretical predictions.
steps.searchable_summary: compare experimental angle measurements with theoretical values

methods.description: This method involves counting the number of spines on the pollen grain and calculating the spine index and D-spine index to understand the morphological characteristics of the pollen.

steps.description: Count the number of spines on the pollen grain surface using electron microscopy pictures.
steps.searchable_summary: count spines on pollen grain using electron microscopy images

steps.description: Calculate the spine index as the ratio between the height and the width of the pollen spine.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate spine index from spine height and width

steps.description: Introduce and calculate the D-spine index as the ratio between the radius of the pollen grain and a spine height on average.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate D-spine index from pollen grain radius and average spine height
